codeReview.summary: The fix directly closes the prior browser-side restart-cycle gap by extending VAL-REG-004 through stop -> restart in the timer-page E2E. The original feature's backend proof was already in place, and this follow-up commit adds the missing browser evidence that the current timer surface and list-view history stay aligned after the restart boundary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-regression-worker skill still requires an agent-browser manual replay after automated tests pass, but both the original worker session and this fix session reported followedProcedure=true with no interactiveChecks and no agent-browser usage in the transcript skeletons.

summary: Pass: reviewed the original and fix diffs plus both transcript skeletons. The original failure was the missing browser restart-cycle proof, and commit 5789bc4 resolves it by extending the VAL-REG-004 E2E through stop -> restart so browser-visible current-timer and history consistency are now covered at the restart boundary.
